algorithms.title: Medical Patient Airway Management Sequence
algorithms.description: Assessing and supporting the airway and ventilation in a medical patient.
steps: Assess rate, depth, effort, color, and level of consciousness — not rate alone.
If apnea, bradypnea, inadequate tidal volume, cyanosis, or altered LOC with poor effort: begin BVM ventilation.
Select an adjunct: OPA if unresponsive with no gag reflex; NPA if gag reflex present or semi-conscious.
Avoid NPA if basilar skull fracture or significant mid-face trauma is suspected.
If vomit, blood, or fluid is present: turn the patient onto their side (or logroll with spinal precautions) and suction.
If stridor is present: treat as an urgent, potentially rapidly progressing airway emergency and prepare for advanced airway support.
